meta.title: Blog — Writing on Structured Systems and Developer Tooling
meta.description: Read essays by Andrew Linh on schema-driven content, end-to-end type safety, AI agent tooling, developer experience, and practical backend architecture.

data.label: Blog
data.title: Essays on structured systems, developer tooling, and architectural clarity
data.description: Writing here focuses on the contracts, runtime behaviors, and organizational choices that make software easier to build and operate.

data.label: All posts
data.title: Recent articles
data.description: Long-form notes drawn from consulting work, platform design, and technical editorial practice.

data.label: Editorial stance
data.title: What the blog tries to do
data.description: I prefer writing that turns architecture into practical decision-making rather than abstract inspiration.

items.title: Specific over vague
items.body: Concrete examples, explicit trade-offs, and operational consequences matter more than broad best-practice slogans.

items.title: Systems over tools
items.body: I care less about novelty than about how a tool changes ownership, failure modes, and maintenance burden.

items.title: Writing as infrastructure
items.body: Good technical writing reduces ambiguity in exactly the same way good interfaces do.

data.label: Commissioned writing
data.title: Need a technical article, architecture memo, or internal platform narrative?
data.description: I also work with teams on developer-facing documentation, system explainers, and editorial framing for technically complex products.
